phases.name: Pre-Battle
phases.flavour: Before the first turn — muster, generate spells, set the field and deploy.

subs.name: Muster & Spells
subs.intro: Agree the game, muster both armies to the points limit, then each Wizard generates the spells they know.

items: Agree the points limit, scenario and any house rules.
Each player musters their army to the limit (build it in the Army tab).
Reveal your army lists to one another.
Each Wizard generates the spells they know from their chosen Lore of Magic.
Note any abilities used ‘before the battle’ or at the ‘start of the first turn’.

subs.name: Set up the Battlefield
subs.intro: Set up the table together: place terrain, then work out table sides and deployment zones.

items: Use a 6′×4′ table, or as the scenario states.
Place terrain together following the scenario / ‘how much terrain’ guidance.
Determine table sides and each player’s deployment zone.

subs.name: Deploy Armies
subs.intro: Alternate setting up units within your deployment zone, then resolve Scouts, Vanguard and any reserve moves.

items: Roll off; the winner chooses who sets up the first unit.
Alternate deploying one unit (with its characters) at a time, inside your zone.
War machines, weapon teams and the like each deploy as a single choice.
Once both armies are down, set up any Scouts.
Make Vanguard moves — the player who finished deploying first moves (and gets +1 for first turn).

subs.name: Determine First Turn
subs.intro: Decide who takes the first turn, resolve any pre-game abilities, then begin Round 1.

items: The player who finished deploying first may take the first turn, or both roll off (+1 to that player).
Resolve any ‘start of the first turn’ or ‘before the battle’ abilities.
Begin Round 1 → advance to the Strategy phase.

phases.name: Strategy
phases.flavour: Stir your host to action — command, conjure, and rally the wavering.

subs.name: Start of Turn
subs.intro: Resolve any start-of-turn special rules and tests before the turn begins in earnest.

items: Resolve any 'start of turn' special rules on your units.
Make any tests required at the start of the turn.
Check scenario victory conditions before playing on, if required.
Tidy up: remove stray casualties and reset loose dice.
Ask your opponent any rules or magic item questions now.

subs.name: Command
subs.intro: Characters unleash powerful abilities, both magical and mundane.

items: Choose a model (usually a character) that is not fleeing.
State which Command special rule it will use, if any.
Name affected unit(s), then make any required tests.
Repeat for every eligible model with a Command special rule.

items.t: Unless stated otherwise, a model can use each special rule only once per Command sub-phase.

subs.name: Conjuration
subs.intro: Wizards cast Enchantment and Hex spells. See how casting and dispelling work.

items: Choose one of your Wizards that is not fleeing.
Attempt to cast any Enchantment or Hex spells it knows.
If cast successfully, the inactive player may attempt to dispel.
Repeat for every Wizard in your army.

subs.name: Rally Fleeing Troops
subs.intro: Attempt to rally any of your units that are fleeing.

items: Identify each of your units that is currently fleeing.
Make a Rally test for a chosen fleeing unit, testing against Leadership.
If passed, the unit rallies and stops fleeing.
If failed, the unit continues to flee.
Repeat until every fleeing unit has tested to rally.

items.t: A rallied unit may make a free reform, but cannot move further or shoot this turn unless stated.

phases.name: Movement
phases.flavour: Regiments wheel and march across the field of war.

subs.name: Declare Charges
subs.intro: Nominate which units will charge, then let your opponent declare a reaction for each target.

items: Declare which units will charge, one at a time.
Nominate an enemy unit as the target of each charge.
Check the target is in the charging unit's forward arc.
Confirm a charging model can see its target.
Let the opponent declare a charge reaction for each charge.

items.t: A unit that is fleeing, already engaged, or that rallied this turn cannot declare a charge.
The target may Hold, Flee, or Stand & Shoot in response to a declared charge.

blocks.text: For each declared charge, the defender picks one reaction before the charger moves.

items.name: Hold
items.desc: Stand firm and receive the charge in good order.

items.name: Stand & Shoot
items.desc: Loose a volley if the charger began beyond its Movement.

items.name: Flee
items.desc: Turn and flee 2D6" away from the charger.

subs.name: Charge Moves
subs.intro: Move your successful chargers into base contact with their targets.

items: Make a Charge roll: roll 2D6 and add the unit's Movement.
Move the charging unit into base contact with its target.
Make a single wheel as needed to maximise models in contact.
Align the units so the fighting ranks meet.
If the roll is too short, the charge fails.

items.t: Maximum possible charge range equals the unit’s Movement characteristic plus 12.
A failed charger moves its full Charge roll distance straight ahead, then may not shoot this turn.

subs.name: Compulsory Moves
subs.intro: Resolve the movement you don't control before your free moves.

items: Resolve all compulsory moves before any remaining (free) moves.
Move fleeing units first, then other compulsory movers.
Move each unit as directed by its special rule.
Resolve your compulsory moves in any order you choose.

items.t: Units that failed to rally this turn continue to flee during this sub-phase.

subs.name: Remaining Moves
subs.intro: Move everything that is left on the field, then cast Conveyances.

items: Move any units that have not yet moved this turn.
Move each unit up to its Movement characteristic.
March at up to double Movement if no enemy is nearby.
Reform or change formation as permitted instead of moving.
Cast Conveyance spells during this sub-phase.

items.t: A unit cannot march if there is an enemy unit within 8" at the start of its move.
A unit that marches cannot shoot later this turn.

phases.name: Shooting
phases.flavour: Volleys of arrows and shot soar across the battle line.

subs.name: Choose Unit & Target
subs.intro: Pick a unit that can shoot; check line of sight and range, then declare the target.

items: Choose one of your units that is able to shoot.
Check the firing models' line of sight to the enemy.
Check each weapon's range to the target.
Declare which enemy unit is the target.
Determine how many models can shoot.

items.t: Only models with line of sight and the target in range may fire.

subs.name: Roll To Hit
subs.intro: Roll to hit, applying range, movement and cover modifiers.

items: Roll a D6 for each shot being fired.
Compare each roll to the firing model's Ballistic Skill.
Apply any To Hit modifiers to the rolls.
Set aside each dice that scores a hit.

items.t: Apply -1 to hit for moving and shooting, and -1 for firing at long range.
A target behind partial cover gives -1 to hit; full cover gives -2 to hit.

subs.name: Roll To Wound & Saves
subs.intro: Roll to wound; the target makes armour and ward saves.

items: Roll to wound for each hit, comparing Strength to Toughness.
Set aside each dice that scores a wound.
Let the target roll armour saves against each wound.
Apply the Armour Piercing modifier to the save.
Let the target attempt any Ward saves that apply.

items.t: If the wound roll needed is higher than 6, the target cannot be wounded.

subs.name: Remove Casualties & Panic
subs.intro: Remove the slain and test for panic.

items: Total the unsaved wounds inflicted on the target.
Remove one model per unsaved wound as a casualty.
Check whether the unit must take a Panic test.
Take the Panic test against the unit's Leadership.

items.t: A unit that loses 25% or more of its models tests for Panic.
A unit that fails its Panic test will Fall Back in Good Order or Flee.

subs.name: Special Cases
subs.intro: Unusual shooting situations: spells, shooting into combat, automatic hits and more.

items: Resolve Magic Missile and Magical Vortex spells as shooting attacks.
Apply special rules for shooting into combat where allowed.
Let extra ranks shoot when a rule permits it.
Resolve automatic hits without rolling to hit.
Apply hits that inflict multiple wounds.
Remove models slain by instant kills.
Resolve any other unusual attacks as described.

phases.name: Combat
phases.flavour: Steel meets steel as the lines crash together.

subs.name: Choose Combat & Who Fights
subs.intro: Pick a combat to resolve, then work out which models can fight.

items: Choose one combat to resolve from those engaged.
Identify which models are in base contact with the enemy.
Include the fighting rank: models in base contact may fight.
Add supporting attacks from the rank directly behind.
Resolve each chosen combat fully before picking another.

items.t: Models in the rank behind a fighting rank may make a single supporting attack.

subs.name: Order of Attacks
subs.intro: Count attacks and fight in Initiative order; charging units strike first.

items: Count each model’s Attacks characteristic to find attacks made.
Work out the order of attacks by Initiative, highest first.
Resolve attacks in descending Initiative order.
Roll simultaneously for models with the same Initiative.

items.t: Models that charged this turn fight with a +1 Initiative bonus in the first round.

subs.name: Multiple Units
subs.intro: How to handle combats involving more than two units.

items: Determine which enemy units each model is in base contact with.
Allocate each model’s attacks against units it touches.
Divide attacks between multiple enemy units if in contact with several.
Declare how attacks are split before rolling to hit.

subs.name: Roll To Hit
subs.intro: Roll to hit using the Weapon Skill of attacker against defender.

items: Roll a D6 for each attack made.
Compare the roll to the attacker's and defender's Weapon Skill.
Apply any To Hit modifiers.
Set aside each dice that scores a hit.

subs.name: Roll To Wound & Saves
subs.intro: Roll to wound; the foe makes armour saves.

items: Roll to wound for each hit, comparing Strength to Toughness.
Set aside each dice that scores a wound.
Let the defender make armour saves against each wound.
Apply the Armour Piercing modifier to the save.
Let the defender attempt any Ward saves that apply.

subs.name: Remove Casualties
subs.intro: Remove the slain, step forward and close in; weak combats may continue.

items: Total the unsaved wounds inflicted on each unit.
Remove one model per unsaved wound as a casualty.
Remove casualties from the rear rank of the unit.
Keep a count of wounds caused for the combat result.

items.t: As front-rank models are slain, rear-rank models step forward to fill gaps.

subs.name: Combat Result
subs.intro: Total each side's combat result score to find the winner.

items: Total each side's combat result score.
Add 1 point per unsaved wound inflicted.
Add the Rank Bonus: +1 per extra rank up to the maximum.
Add bonuses for a standard, flank or rear attack, and high ground.
Compare totals; the higher score wins the combat.

items.t: Claim +1 per extra rank behind the first, up to a maximum set by troop type.

subs.name: Result — Multiple Combats
subs.intro: Bonuses when several units are locked in the same combat.

items: Total the combat result across all units on each side.
Combine the wounds inflicted by every unit involved.
Count each bonus once for the side, as described.
Compare the combined scores to find the winning side.

subs.name: Break Test
subs.intro: The loser tests to hold; failure means breaking and fleeing.

items: The side that lost the combat must take a Break test.
Roll a Leadership test with a modifier equal to the result difference.
If passed, the unit holds its nerve.
If failed, the unit Breaks and Flees.

items.t: Apply a penalty equal to the difference in the combat result scores.

subs.name: Follow Up & Pursuit
subs.intro: The winner may follow up, pursue, or overrun fleeing foes.

items: Resolve follow up moves when a beaten enemy gives ground.
Pursue an enemy that Breaks and Flees from combat.
Roll for pursuit distance as described in the rules.
Move pursuers into contact, or overrun if no enemy remains.

items.t: A unit may attempt to restrain pursuit, and may reform instead of pursuing.

subs.name: Special Cases
subs.intro: Spells in combat and other unusual combat situations.

items: Resolve Assailment spells cast during the Combat phase.
Handle shrinking units that lose models mid-combat.
Apply rules when no more foes remain in base contact.
Resolve combats involving incomplete ranks or split profiles.
Handle models fighting with different weapons.

subs.name: Terrain & Combat
subs.intro: How terrain and obstacles affect a fight.

items: Note how terrain affects the units in this combat.
Apply effects of open ground, hills and difficult terrain.
Resolve combats fought across linear obstacles.
Apply rules for dangerous or impassable terrain and woods.

subs.name: End of Turn
subs.intro: Once every combat is resolved, the turn ends and play passes to your opponent.

items: Confirm all combats have been resolved.
Resolve any 'end of turn' special rules or effects.
End your turn and pass play to your opponent.
